title: heliamphora elongata
intro: Heliamphora elongata, also known as the elongated sundew or long-leaved pitcher plant, is a carnivorous plant species belonging to the family Droseraceae. Native to South America, specifically in the Guyana Shield, it obtains essential nutrients by capturing and digesting small insects. The plant's leaves contain iridoids and other compounds that exhibit potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Traditional herbal preparations of Heliamphora elongata involve infusions and tinctures, which may be used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
